title: trisyngyne grandis
parts: Trisyngyne grandis has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and stems, which are utilized for their therapeutic properties. The leaves of Trisyngyne grandis are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The roots of this plant contain saponins and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and hepatoprotective activities. The stems of Trisyngyne grandis are also known to contain alkaloids, such as berberine and palmatine, which have been used to treat various diseases, including fever and digestive issues.
side_effects: Trisyngyne grandis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea, headaches, and dizziness due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to these compounds can also lead to liver damage and kidney failure in extreme cases. In addition, improper use of the plant can result in an allergic reaction, characterized by itching, redness, and swelling of the skin. Furthermore, consuming the plant's berries can cause stomach pain, diarrhea, and vomiting due to its high levels of alkaloids.
